bead.title: Implement consumer workload runner core
bead.description:
Implement the core autonomous consumer-workload runner for Axon after the contract bead defines status semantics and evidence layout.

The runner should be able to execute a self-contained fake workload without requiring sibling repos. It must create an isolated run directory, emit summary.json, capture command logs, classify command outcomes, and expose dry-run/self-test modes so CI can validate the harness cheaply. It must be written so later beads can add Nexiq, DDx, and Cayce adapters without rewriting the core.

Required behavior:
- scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh accepts at least --consumer, --backend, --dry-run, --self-test, and --mode or an equivalent documented option set.
- The runner records Axon git SHA, backend, consumer name, status, failure classification, command list, log paths, started_at/finished_at, and exit code in summary.json.
- Dry-run must print or write the commands/env that would run without starting real services.
- Self-test must exercise the evidence writer and status classifier using a fake consumer command.
- Process cleanup must be handled with trap/finally behavior so child processes are stopped.

In-scope files:
- scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh
- tests/test_consumer_workload_runner.py or an equivalent lightweight test file under tests/
- docs/helix/03-test/consumer-workload-gate.md only if the implementation discovers a contract detail that must be clarified.

Out of scope:
- Running Nexiq, DDx, or Cayce real commands.
- Adding GitHub Actions workflow wiring.
- Requiring Docker or Postgres in self-test.
bead.acceptance:
1. `bash -n scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh` passes.
2. `python3 tests/test_consumer_workload_runner.py` passes.
3. `scripts/run-consumer-workloads.sh --self-test` exits 0 and writes a summary.json containing the keys `consumer`, `backend`, `status`, `classification`, `commands`, and `axon_sha`.
